t_ch: 这就是整件事情不寻常的地方了。
t_en: That's what makes this entire thing so unusual.

t_ch: 这到底有什么不寻常的地方嘛？他们不就是像列车组那样收到了来自景元的邀请？
t_en: What's so unusual about it? They simply received an invitation from Jing Yuan, just like the Crew, right?

t_ch: 演武仪典不过是一场小小的节庆，能让其他仙舟的两位天将同时来到这儿，只怕另有原因。
t_en: The Wardance is just a small festival, and now we have two Arbiter-Generals from other Xianzhou ships here. I'm afraid they're here for something more.

t_ch: 他们是为了罗浮建木灾异一事前来问责的吧。
t_en: Maybe they've come to hold Jing Yuan accountable for the Ambrosial Arbor Crisis?

t_ch: 问责？不至于吧。药王秘传的坏家伙和反物质军团入侵罗浮大搞破坏，罗浮不是受害者吗？哪有受害者被问责的道理啊？
t_en: Hold him accountable? Come on, didn't the Luofu fall victim to the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus and the Antimatter Legion? Why would they blame the victim?

t_ch: 丹枢的叛乱、幻胧的计划…在联盟其他天将眼中只是一面之词。只有一样铁证被留了下来，遗患无穷。
t_en: Dan Shu's rebellion and Phantylia's scheming are merely one side of the story to the other Arbiter-Generals of the Alliance. Only a single piece of incontrovertible evidence remains, creating an endless source of potential complications.

t_ch: 建木。
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor.

t_ch: 罗浮仙舟压制许久的寿瘟祸迹再度重生，是不争的事实。但究竟是反物质军团的阴谋策动，还是罗浮内部起了叛心，连景元本人也涉入其中？
t_en: Yes, it's undeniable that the plaguemark, which was subdued by the Xianzhou Luofu, has resurfaced. But was it really a conspiracy instigated by the Antimatter Legion, or does it indicate a traitorous intent from within the Luofu, implicating Jing Yuan himself?

t_ch: 猜疑的火一旦点燃，就很难被熄灭了。
t_en: Once the spark of suspicion is kindled, it proves hard to extinguish.

t_ch: 我们倒是能为景元作证。
t_en: We can vouch for Jing Yuan, though.

t_ch: 将军邀请我们前来演武仪典观礼，想必也有这一层考虑。
t_en: The general must have had that in mind when he invited us to attend the Wardance.

t_ch: 难怪咱们被叫回来观礼。
t_en: No wonder we were called back for the ceremony.

t_ch: 我真傻，真的。原以为能有一场说走就走，快快乐乐的旅行。现在看来，到哪儿星穹列车都脱不开是非啊。
t_en: What was I thinking, seriously. Here I was looking forward to a carefree and enjoyable trip, but it seems wherever we go, drama is just around the corner.

t_ch: 可恶，我一开始竟还真以为天将们只是前来观礼，还满心欢喜呢……
t_en: Aww... I was so excited. I thought those Arbiter-Generals were just here to see the ceremony...

t_ch: 如此想来，我听说这一次随朱明使节舰而来的还有一位丹士，据说会出任罗浮丹鼎司的新任司鼎。
t_en: By the way, I heard that an alchemist from the Zhuming diplomatic ship has arrived, and rumors say that she's to be the new Cauldron Master of the Alchemy Commission on the Luofu.

t_ch: 朱明派来的人，担任罗浮的司鼎？倒是有先例，只是这时机……
t_en: An alchemist from the Zhuming serving as the Cauldron Master on the Luofu? While it's not unheard of, the timing itself...

t_ch: 听丹恒先生一说，彦卿才察觉到这背后涌动的暗流。
t_en: Thanks to your words, Mr. Dan Heng, now I finally see the underlying tensions.

t_ch: 将军此时正承担着莫大的压力。可我竟全然不察将军的苦恼…真是幼稚！
t_en: The general is under tremendous pressure right now, but I was completely oblivious to his troubles... How naive of me!

t_ch: 你也别这么想嘛！大人们的事本来就该大人们来操心。何况眼下你能为你家将军做些什么呢？
t_en: Come on! Don't think like that! Leave the adult matters to the adults. Even if you wanted to do something for the general, it's not like you can do anything.

t_ch: 呃，我是不是又说错话了。
t_en: Uh, did I say something wrong again?

t_ch: 论说话水平，还得是你！
t_en: You're a real master of conversation!

t_ch: 三月，你就少说点吧。
t_en: Please, March, just keep quiet.

t_ch: 三月小姐说的没错，彦卿本事低微，眼下还不能为将军分忧。
t_en: Miss March is right. I don't have the skills to share the general's burdens at the moment.

t_ch: …但我可以尽力做好将军交代的每一件事。我们走吧，在将三位的住宿安排妥当后，我还要去工造司处理公司的抗议。
t_en: ...Still, I'll do my best to follow his instructions. Let's go. Once I've taken you to your accommodations, I need to go to the Artisanship Commission to handle the IPC's protest.

t_ch: 看他这心事重重的样子，我还真有点放心不下…我们陪你一起去工造司吧！
t_en: He looks like he has a lot on his mind. We can't just let him go alone... Why don't we accompany you to the Artisanship Commission?

t_ch: 这…不妥吧。三位愿意帮忙，我很感激，但公司职员格外难缠…我担心反倒让列车惹上麻烦……
t_en: This... is too much trouble. While I appreciate your kindness, dealing with the IPC workers could be tricky... I'm afraid this will cause trouble for the Express...

t_ch: 你放心好了！对付公司的人，我们列车组可相当有经验！「石心十人」你听过吧？我们和其中好几个都打过交道！对吧，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: No worries! We're pretty experienced in dealing with the IPC! You've heard of the Ten Stonehearts, right? We've dealt with quite a few of them, right, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 你乱吹牛别带上我。
t_en: Brag all you want, but don't drag me into this.

t_ch: 谁乱吹牛了！几个公司职员，难道比砂金还厉害？不能够吧～
t_en: Hey, who's bragging? Anyway, this time it's just a few ordinary IPC workers. Surely you don't think they'll be even trickier than Aventurine, do you?

t_ch: 论说话水平，还得是你！
t_en: You're a real master of conversation!

t_ch: 就是就是…呃，你这话听着不像好话。
t_en: Yeah, exactly... Uhm, are you being sarcastic?

t_ch: 别别别，我怕惹上官司。
t_en: Stop, stop! I don't want to end up in an IPC lawsuit.

t_ch: 你怎么越来越胆小了？你砸坏人家大铁人的时候也没见你怕吃官司。
t_en: What's with you turning into a scaredy-cat? I didn't see you worrying about lawsuits when you wrecked that giant mech.

t_ch: 好吧，既然老师们有意帮忙，那我也不再推辞。去工造司会会他们吧。
t_en: Well, since you're willing to help, I won't decline your kindness. Let's head to the Artisanship Commission and meet them.

t_ch: 各位！我瞧见你们了。这边！
t_en: There you are! Everyone, this way!

t_ch: 等等，我的剑！
t_en: Wait, my sword!

t_ch: 还是先办正事要紧。
t_en: Let's just get down to business first.

t_ch: 炎老，舟车劳顿，真是辛苦您了。
t_en: It's been a long journey, Elder Huaiyan. Thank you for your presence.

t_ch: 哎，不辛苦，倒是劳你拨冗迎接了。
t_en: Heh, don't mention it. Thank you for taking the time to welcome me.

t_ch: 这些家伙真是可怕…
t_en: Those guys are terrifying...

t_ch: 将犯人解往幽囚狱。
t_en: Deliver the prisoners to The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 你们凭什么扣押舰船？
t_en: How dare you detain our ship? Who do you think you are?

t_ch: 照章办事罢了。
t_en: We're just following the rules.

t_ch: 步离人？
t_en: Borisin?

t_ch: 为何会在这儿出现步离人？
t_en: Why are there borisin here?

t_ch: 休得猖狂！
t_en: This ends now!

t_ch: （这阴阳怪气的声音实在耳熟…似乎在<color=#dbc291ff>金人巷</color>里听到过？）
t_en: (That toxic voice sounds familiar... Haven't I heard it before in <color=#dbc291ff>Aurum Alley</color>?)

t_ch: 我也不是没和你们天舶司打过交道，你们那刁难人的行事作风我早就习惯了。
t_en: You know what? This isn't my first time dealing with the Sky-Faring Commission, I can handle your unreasonable ways.

t_ch: 但现在你们直接明抢公司的货物，这是不是有点过分了？
t_en: But straight-up snatching IPC cargo, isn't that going a bit too far?

t_ch: 都说了很多次了，只要开箱完成安全检查，我们自然会放行。你是耳朵出了问题，还是脑袋不利索。
t_en: Just as I've said it many times already. Once we've inspected the cargo and completed the security check, you can be on your way. Is there something wrong with your ears, or is it just your brain?

t_ch: 我听得很清楚，也想得很明白！我的话说得更直白：没戏！
t_en: I'm hearing you loud and clear! I'm thinking clear! And my answer is crystal clear: Not a chance!

t_ch: 再扣着我的货物不放，我一纸诉状直接告到你们将军那里去！
t_en: Keep detaining my cargo, and I'll file a complaint directly with your general!

t_ch: 吵什么呢？
t_en: What's all this arguing about?

t_ch: 你们叫多少人来都不可能说服我！嗯？！怎么是你啊？！
t_en: Don't think you can possibly convince me! Uh huh!? Why are you here!?

t_ch: 斯科特，怎么是你啊！
t_en: Skott, what are you doing here!?

t_ch: 我还想问你呢！怎么又遇见你了！
t_en: I was gonna ask you the same thing! Why do I keep running into you!?

t_ch: 似乎听见了狗叫。
t_en: Did I just hear a dog barking?

t_ch: 混蛋！你说谁是狗？怎么…又是你啊？！
t_en: You jerk! Who are you calling a "dog?" Wait... wh—why are you here!?

t_ch: 你这家伙是赖在仙舟不走了是吧？真是祸不单行啊，遇上你准没好事……
t_en: You're staying on the Xianzhou, are you? What terrible luck. Wherever you go, disasters aren't far behind...

t_ch: 这不是星穹列车的客人吗？怎么和彦卿一起来工造司了？
t_en: Aren't you the guests from the Astral Express? What brings you and Yanqing to the Artisanship Commission?

t_ch: 听说你们遇到了点麻烦。
t_en: I heard you're in trouble.

t_ch: 他们遇到了点麻烦？哼，是我！我遇到了麻烦！
t_en: They're in trouble? No, it's me! I'm the one in trouble!

t_ch: 陪彦卿解决公司的麻烦。
t_en: I'm here to help Yanqing sort out the trouble caused by the IPC.

t_ch: 公司的麻烦？该说是天舶司给我们造成的麻烦才对！
t_en: Trouble caused by the IPC? I'd say it's caused by the Sky-Faring Commission!

t_ch: 看来这位公司员工是{NICKNAME}老师的旧识…也不知这算是好事还是坏事。
t_en: Looks like you've met this IPC worker before, {NICKNAME}... I'm not sure if that's a good or bad thing.

t_ch: 夕葵姐姐，我被派来解决公司的抗议，眼下到底是什么情况？
t_en: I was sent here to deal with the IPC protest, Miss Xikui. What's going on here?

t_ch: 如你所知，朱明使节舰救下了这艘被步离人袭击的公司运输船，然后知会云骑军辇道卫，将它带回港口完成修复与检查。
t_en: As you know, this IPC transport ship was attacked by the borisin and rescued by the Zhuming's diplomatic ship. Then the Cloud Knights were instructed to bring it back to the dock for repairs and inspections.

t_ch: 这位，就是运输船的负责人，斯科特先生。
t_en: And this is Mr. Skott, the person in charge of this transport ship.

t_ch: 原来你就是斯科特啊！我听{NICKNAME}提起过你诶～你不是被赶出罗浮了吗？怎么又回来了？
t_en: So, you're Skott! I've heard {NICKNAME} mention you! Weren't you kicked off the Luofu before? Why did you come back?

t_ch: 你以为我想回来吗！我原本以为只是进港维修一番，就能离开这晦气的地方。谁知道一进港就冲上来几个云骑，把货舱里的货物都抢走了！
t_en: Like I wanted to come back! I thought I'd just dock at the harbor for repairs and leave this forsaken place for good. Little did I know, as soon as the ship entered the harbor, a bunch of Cloud Knights showed up and snatched all our cargo from the hold!

t_ch: 什么叫「抢走」？说了多少遍，这是安全检查。
t_en: What do you mean by "snatched"? I've told you a million times. It's a security check.

t_ch: 那怎么又拉到工造司来了？还找来个獐头鼠目的匠人，这不明摆着要偷学公司的专利技术吗？
t_en: Then why did you bring the cargo to the Artisanship Commission? You even brought in some shady craftsman. It's obvious that you're trying to steal the IPC's patented technology!

t_ch: 我说这位…远道而来的客人，第一，天舶司检查到你们的货物中存在疑似危险武器的物品，所以才要求我来进行确认检查。第二，我到底哪里獐头鼠目了？！
t_en: Listen here... Firstly, the Sky-Faring Commission detected dangerous items that could possibly be weapons in your cargo hold, that's why they called me here to double-check. Secondly, where the heck did you get the idea that I'm a "shady craftsman"!?

t_ch: 我就纳闷了，就算是危险武器，关你们屁事啊，又不是运往罗浮？我们修好船就走，不会在这儿卸货的。
t_en: Even if there are dangerous items, what do they have to do with you? It's not even being shipped to the Luofu. We'll just fix the ship and be on our way, we won't unload our cargo here.

t_ch: 但你们要在港口停留数日，才能修复引擎再次启航。我们怎么可能让未经检查的危险品在罗浮港口停留数日呢？
t_en: But you'll have to stay in the port for several days before your ship is repaired and you can take off again. How can we just leave unchecked items sitting here?

t_ch: 我明白了…安全检查应该不需要拆解货物吧？
t_en: I understand... but we don't need to disassemble the cargo if it's just a security check, right?

t_ch: 本来不需要。我们原本只做了些进一步的扫描检测。但，扫描结果却发现货箱中存放的不只有机械结构，还有一些…近似生物组织的特征。
t_en: In most cases, we don't. However, our scans discovered that the cargo doesn't only contain machinery but also some... substance that resembles biological tissue.

t_ch: 生物组织？这箱子里装着活物？
t_en: Biological tissue? Does this crate contain living things?

t_ch: 具体的结论恐怕要等丹鼎司的人来确认。不管怎样，按照条例，我们都要启封其中一个货品进一步检查。但这位公司专员却用专利保密的理由一再阻挠。
t_en: I'm afraid we'll need to wait for the Alchemy Commission for further confirmation. In any case, according to our regulations, we need to unseal one of the crates for further examination, but this IPC specialist has been hindering us on the grounds of patent secrecy.

t_ch: 联盟对生物制品的管理条例非常严格。如果不进一步检查，天舶司是无论如何都不可能放行的。
t_en: The Alliance's regulations on biological products are very strict. Without further inspection, there is no way for the Sky-Faring Commission to release the cargo.

t_ch: 好啊，谁想动这批货物，我斯科特就和谁拼命！不管我们运的东西是生物还是机械，都和你们没有关系！我要投诉天舶司的处理程序！
t_en: Oh, really? Fine! If anyone lays a finger on that shipment, they'll have me to deal with! It doesn't matter if it's mechanical or biological, it's none of your business! I'm filing a complaint against the Sky-Faring Commission's ridiculous regulations!

t_ch: 看来这位斯科特先生非常顽固，不是很容易说服。唉…彦卿实在不希望和公司闹到撕破脸的地步……
t_en: This Mr. Skott seems stubborn and difficult to persuade. Honestly... I really don't want to have a vicious confrontation with the IPC...

t_ch: 我倒是有说服斯科特的经验…
t_en: Well, I know a thing or two about convincing Skott...

t_ch: 交给我吧！我是谈判专家！
t_en: Leave it to me! I'm an expert negotiator!

t_ch: 我听说过{NICKNAME}之前帮助金人巷的事情。当时公司方面的代表，就是这位斯科特先生吧？既然你有对付他的经验…看来这次也得麻烦{NICKNAME}老师了。
t_en: I heard how you helped Aurum Alley, {NICKNAME}. The IPC representative back then was Mr. Skott, right? Since you've dealt with him before... it looks like I'll have to rely on you again, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你们在那里嘀嘀咕咕什么呢？快点把货物还给我们！
t_en: What are you guys whispering about over there? Just hurry up and give us back our cargo!

t_ch: （我记得这家伙的性格<color=#dbc291ff>属于典型的吃硬不吃软，但姑且还算是遵守规则。</color>利用他的性格特点来对付他吧。）
t_en: (As I recall, this guy <color=#dbc291ff>won't listen to reason and can only be persuaded with intimidation. But he does seem to follow rules to some extent.</color> Let's use that against him.)

t_ch: 既然你们搬出安全规章，那咱们就来谈谈法律。按照《仙舟联盟-星际和平公司贸易共识宣言》第四款之规定，联盟和公司绝不能侵犯对方的知识产权。
t_en: Speaking of regulations, we have our own laws and regulations too. According to Article 4 of the Xianzhou Alliance-IPC Trade Consensus, the Alliance and IPC shall never infringe on each other's intellectual property rights.

t_ch: 依照法律提出建议
t_en: Make proposals from the legal perspective.

t_ch: 联盟可以和你签署保密协议，这样就不用担心他们窥探了吧？
t_en: The Alliance can sign a non-disclosure agreement with you. That way, you won't have to worry about any infringements, right?

t_ch: 我们可以按照公司的规矩，签署一份双方都能接受的保密协议。
t_en: We can sign a mutually acceptable non-disclosure agreement in accordance with the IPC's rules.

t_ch: 话…话是这么说，可谁知道你们会不会遵守！
t_en: Well, th—that makes sense, but... how can we trust you to honor the terms?

t_ch: 抬出彦卿威胁
t_en: Use Yanqing's identity to intimidate him.

t_ch: 之前在金人巷你面对的是民间商会，你可以抬出公司来吓唬他们。
t_en: Back in Aurum Alley, you were dealing with civilian merchants, where you could use the IPC's name to scare them.

t_ch: 但眼前这位云骑骁卫代表了罗浮将军的意志，不会被你几句话唬住。
t_en: But this time, the Cloud Knight lieutenant standing before you represents the will of Luofu's general and won't be intimidated by your words.

t_ch: 当然，我相信我们双方都希望事情能和平解决。
t_en: Of course, I'm sure we both want a peaceful resolution.

t_ch: 说得不错，我当然希望事情能顺利解决，但眼下这不是被僵化的手续卡住了脖子吗？
t_en: Yeah, that's true. Of course, I want things to be resolved peacefully, but aren't we getting stuck in rigid formalities here?

t_ch: 先恶心他一下再说
t_en: Start by making him feel uncomfortable.

t_ch: 没想到还能和你再相见，斯科特先生～我还挺怀念咱们在金人巷的对手戏～也许这就是我们之间的缘分吧。
t_en: I didn't expect to see you again, Mr. Skott! We had such fun in Aurum Alley last time. It seems our paths are destined to cross once more.

t_ch: …少套近乎！要不是我打不过你，非给你两拳不可！你们到底解决不解决问题？还是想让这小丑在这儿继续丢人现眼？
t_en: ...Stop pretending we're friends! If I could actually beat you, I'd punch you in the face! So, are you guys going to resolve this problem? Or are you just going to let this clown put on a show here?

t_ch: 丹恒，我都想假装不认识{F#她}{M#他}了。
t_en: Let's just pretend we don't know {F#her}{M#him}, Dan Heng.

t_ch: 让他回忆起难堪往事
t_en: Remind him of his embarrassing past.

t_ch: 给诸位介绍一下，这位是我的老朋友斯科特。他多才多艺，尤其擅长模仿动物朋友。
t_en: Hey, everyone! Meet my old friend, Skott. He's very talented, especially at imitating animals.

t_ch: 住…住口！谈事就谈事，你翻那些旧账有意思吗？
t_en: S-stop it! Let's focus on the business at hand. What's the point in dragging up the past?

t_ch: 哇！好厉害～能教教我吗？你都擅长模仿什么动物呀？
t_en: Whoa, you're amazing! Can you teach me? Which animals can you imitate?

t_ch: 别问了，别问了……
t_en: J—Just forget it...

t_ch: 且不谈知识产权保密，光是这些博识学会制造的测试原型机的造价就高到你们无法想象！要是有什么闪失，用尽你们漫长余生打工也赔不起！
t_en: Even if we set aside the secrecy of intellectual property, these prototypes built by the Intelligentsia Guild are incredibly valuable, beyond your wildest imagination! If anything goes wrong, you won't be able to pay for it even with your lives!

t_ch: 天舶司可以照价赔偿
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission can pay compensation.

t_ch: 斯科特先生能否核算出一个具体金额呢？在检修过后要是有所损坏，列车…呃，不对，是天舶司方面是愿意照价赔偿的。
t_en: Can you give us an exact amount, Mr. Skott? If there's any damage after the inspection, the Express... um, I mean, the Sky-Faring Commission will compensate you.

t_ch: 天舶司…当然…可以赔偿。前提是要能明确出具相关的损坏报告。
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission... Yeah, they will compensate you... provided a detailed report of the damage is submitted.

t_ch: 我不怀疑天舶司的财力…但是！这也不是钱的事儿！
t_en: I don't doubt the financial strength of the Sky-Faring Commission... However! This is not just about money!

t_ch: 向他保证你们会小心行事
t_en: Assure him that you will proceed with caution.

t_ch: 你可别小瞧仙舟的技术。检查过程就算是把货物拆成零件，工造司的人也能给你拼回去。
t_en: Don't underestimate Xianzhou technology. Even if they need to dismantle your cargo, the Artisanship Commission can easily put it back together.

t_ch: 你当然可以把仙舟的本事吹上天，说到底你不过是他们请来的说客罢了。
t_en: Yeah, go ahead and brag about their skills. After all, you're just a negotiator they hired.

t_ch: 但我不一样，作为公司的成员，我的船队接受了这次运输订单，我就必须对这里的每一件货物的完好负责到底。
t_en: But I'm different. I'm an IPC member, and my fleet has accepted this transportation order. Naturally, I need to be responsible for the integrity of every piece of cargo.

t_ch: 赔钱是不可能赔钱的
t_en: Pay you compensation? You wish!

t_ch: 笑话，我拿走过黑塔的奇物，打死过阮•梅的试验品，我赔过一分钱了吗？你两张嘴皮子一碰，就敢来问我要钱？
t_en: Hah! I've taken Herta's curios, and even destroyed Ruan Mei's experimental subjects, but have I ever paid a penny for them? And now you think you can just come and ask me for compensation?

t_ch: 听好了{F#小姑娘}{M#小子}，少拿天才俱乐部的关系来压我。公司和那群高高在上的天才们是各取所需的关系，不是上下级。
t_en: Listen up, brat. Don't think that your fancy connection to the Genius Society scares me. The IPC and those geniuses are working together. We're not taking orders from them.

t_ch: 与其讲道理不如直接发疯
t_en: Just skip the reasoning part and resort to good old violence.

t_ch: 对了，我要是抡起球棒一砸，你就不得不请人来核计损失索赔吧？这样一来，岂不就省去了安全检查？
t_en: I've got a brilliant idea! I'll just smash everything with my bat, and then you can hire someone to assess the damage. That way, your cargo won't need any inspection, right?

t_ch: 冷静，{NICKNAME}。将军希望能妥善处理此事。
t_en: Calm down, {NICKNAME}. The general wants us to handle this peacefully.

t_ch: 这就是你想出来的解决方案？行啊，你干脆从我尸体上踏过去算了。
t_en: So, this is the great idea you came up with? Fine! Why don't you just step over my lifeless body?

t_ch: 另外，这艘运输舰上的货物归属于博识学会。你们要想检查，是不是至少也要召来一名博识学会的委托人在场？
t_en: Besides, the cargo on this transport vessel belongs to the Intelligentsia Guild. If you want to inspect the cargo, shouldn't you at least call in a member of the Intelligentsia Guild to be present?

t_ch: 尝试按照仙舟的法条解决问题
t_en: Try to resolve this according to Xianzhou regulations.

t_ch: 按照属地原则，既然这批货物滞留在仙舟的港口，天舶司当然有权检查。
t_en: According to the principle of territoriality, since your vessel is stranded on the Xianzhou, the Sky-Faring Commission has the right to inspect it.

t_ch: 的确如此。据《仙舟律》港阜卷廿七条所载：「诸官、私物，一入港阜，当受搜检。拒之者以赃论。」
t_en: Indeed. According to Article 27 of the Xianzhou Legal Code: "All official and private goods, upon entering the port, shall be subject to inspection. Failure to submit to such inspection shall be deemed equivalent to the possession of unlawful items and shall be subject to legal ramifications."

t_ch: 啊？什么意思？…我这联觉信标怎么都翻译不出来？
t_en: Huh? My Synesthesia Beacon must not be working... because I have no idea what you just said.

t_ch: 简单来说，就是如果你执意要拒绝我们搜查货物，我们就不得不将它当做赃物收缴了。
t_en: Simply put, if you insist on hindering the inspection, we'll have to treat the cargo as unlawful items and confiscate it.

t_ch: 收缴？！你们…你们仙舟人怎么能这么不讲道理！
t_en: Confiscate it!? How... how can you Xianzhou people be so unreasonable!

t_ch: 斯科特先生，要是在庇尔波因特，入港的货物有安全隐患时，公司也会将它收容处置，没错吧？
t_en: If this were Pier Point, and incoming cargo posed a safety risk, the IPC would take it in for containment and disposal, correct?

t_ch: 呃…说的倒是没错，但、但公司偶尔也会允许一些例外。比如仙舟的舰船，公司向来是乐意网开一面的。
t_en: Umm... th—that's true, but the IPC s—sometimes makes exceptions. For example, they've always given special terms to Xianzhou vessels.

t_ch: 是时候把问题扩大化了！
t_en: It's time to escalate the issue!

t_ch: 东拉西扯，连博识学会的人都被你搬出来了。看来你是毫无解决问题的诚意啊。
t_en: You're pulling out all the stops, and you've even dragged members of the Intelligentsia Guild into this. Seems like you're not interested in resolving this at all.

t_ch: 斯科特先生，看在咱们打过交道的份上，我不妨给你透个底：你们的运输船被步离人袭击，这件事可大可小。
t_en: Since we've met each other before, Mr. Skott, let me share some information. Your transport ship was attacked by the borisin, and the Cloud Knights might see it as more than just a random attack.

t_ch: 呵呵呵，你当我是吓大的吗？可大怎么说，可小又怎么说？
t_en: Hehe, you think you can scare me with that? What exactly do you mean?

t_ch: 往小了说，列位是受步离人劫掠的被害者。但往大了说，为什么步离人要在通往罗浮的航线上袭击你们？莫非船员中有人勾结了步离人？
t_en: Sure, you might purely be innocent victims of a borisin attack, but think about it another way. Why would the borisin launch such an attack near the Luofu? Perhaps some of your crew members colluded with them?

t_ch: 也许需要详加审问全体船员，才能排除这个隐患？
t_en: We may need to interrogate every one of your crew to eliminate this potential risk?

t_ch: …其实吧，学会委托人在场也不是必要条件，我这不是…希望相关的手续更合规齐全嘛。
t_en: ...You know what? I don't think we really need a member from the Intelligentsia Guild present. I'm just hoping... that all the procedures will be thoroughly followed, you know?

t_ch: 尝试按照公司的法条解决问题
t_en: Try to resolve this according to IPC regulations.

t_ch: 既然博识学会将货物交付给公司运输，公司就理应对货物的安全负责？为什么要联系博识学会？
t_en: Since the Intelligentsia Guild entrusted the cargo to the IPC for transportation, it's the IPC's responsibility to ensure its safety, right? Why would you need a member of the Intelligentsia Guild to be present?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，这就是你不懂了～按照公司的《筑材物流部运输合同法》第<unbreak>17</unbreak>条之规定：「当货物已寄出且仍在途中时，除运输事务安全外一切风险责任由寄件方承担。」
t_en: Hehe, let me tell you something! As stated in Article 17 of the IPC's Building Material Logistics Department Transport Agreement Law: "Subsequent to the dispatch of goods and during their transit, all risks, save for those pertaining to the transportation safety, shall be the sole liability of the consignor."

t_ch: 哎！我们只负责保护货物的完整性，防止有心觊觎的人染指。比如…咳咳。
t_en: Well! We only protect the integrity of the goods and prevent anyone with ill intentions from getting their hands on them, for example...

t_ch: 至于货物本身是否会对目的地产生安全威胁，这个责我们可担不起。嘿嘿，触及到你们知识盲区了吧？法学难着呢！
t_en: As for whether the cargo itself poses a security threat to the destination, that's not our responsibility. It appears we have reached an area beyond your expertise, doesn't it? The complexities of legal studies are indeed challenging!

t_ch: 向他表明你广博的人脉
t_en: Show him your extensive connections.

t_ch: 我和博识学会的拉帝奥教授亲如一家。有道是「上阵父子兵」，我出面是不是就算是博识学会出面了？
t_en: I'm pretty tight with Dr. Ratio from the Intelligentsia Guild, almost like family. Doesn't that make me practically a member of the Intelligentsia Guild too?

t_ch: 这都什么乱七八糟的？就算你和他有婚姻关系，你也代表不了学会啊！
t_en: What's all this nonsense? Even if you were married to him, you still couldn't represent the Intelligentsia Guild!

t_ch: 公司和仙舟都是法治社会，得遵守办事流程啊！我身为公司雇员，也必须遵守公司的条令律法。要是破例让你们检查，那真是把天给捅塌了啊。
t_en: We both need to follow the regulations, because that's how the IPC and the Xianzhou operate, right? As an IPC worker, I have to abide by its regulations. If I make an exception and allow you to inspect the cargo, it'll spell disaster for me.

t_ch: 仙舟也只是按规矩走
t_en: The Xianzhou people are just following their own rules.

t_ch: 你有你的规矩，人家有人家的规矩。我们在人家的地盘上，是不是应该守人家的规矩？
t_en: Everyone has their own set of rules. Now that you're on their turf, shouldn't you follow their rules, too?

t_ch: 我当然知道自己站在仙舟的疆域上，但是违反了流程，我回去也是要受处分的啊！
t_en: Yeah, I get it. I know I'm on the Xianzhou, but if I violate the IPC regulations, I'll be in deep trouble when I get back!

t_ch: 谈谈未来黯淡的仕途
t_en: Talk about his unpromising career prospects.

t_ch: 斯科特先生，也许在你看来，你是在捍卫自己身为公司雇员的底线，值得敬佩。可是你不明白，你同时也透支着公司在仙舟的形象。
t_en: I understand that you're trying to stick to the IPC regulations, and that's commendable, Mr. Skott. But what you fail to see is that this argument is tarnishing the IPC's reputation on the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 你与天舶司的冲突一旦激化，不仅会给自己带来更多商业上的不便，也会为你的职业前景带来许多无形的阻碍。
t_en: If this conflict with the Sky-Faring Commission escalates, it won't just make things difficult for your business, but it'll also have an impact on your career down the line.

t_ch: 当然，我…我无意与仙舟发生冲突。但就像我刚开始说的那样，我要求的自始至终只是取回货物，就此离开。是各位不肯放行啊。
t_en: Of course, I... I never wanted conflict with the Xianzhou. Like I said, all I wanted was to retrieve the cargo and be done with it, but they just refuse to let us go.

t_ch: 尝试贿赂他
t_en: Try to bribe him.

t_ch: 这样吧，兄弟，咱们也算是老相识了…你今天给我们行个方便，我们日后少不了你的好处。如何？
t_en: How about this, pal? We go way back... so, if you make things easy for us today, we'll make it worth your while. What do you say?

t_ch: 哪有这么明目张胆行贿的？我要是当着这么多公司兄弟的面，给你行了个方便，这不是等着风纪人员找我麻烦吗！
t_en: Are you seriously trying to bribe me right in front of everyone? If I show you any favoritism, the disciplinary officers will come down on me!

t_ch: 没事，也少不了弟兄们的份！
t_en: Don't worry! They'll get their cut too!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，咱们还是使些合法手段吧。
t_en: Let's just do this legally, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 放低姿态苦苦哀求他
t_en: Swallow your pride and beg him.

t_ch: 求求你了、求求你了，我在朋友们面前夸下海口，说自己可以说服你…你要是不松口，我就没脸在他们面前混下去了！求求你不要这样狠心啊！
t_en: Please, help me! I mean it! I bragged to my friends that I could convince you... If you don't do me this favor, I'll be so embarrassed! Come on, don't be so cold-hearted!

t_ch: 金人巷的时候是我不对，我向你诚挚道歉，求求你大人不记小人过，给我一次洗心革面重新做人的机会吧！
t_en: I'm truly sorry for what I did in Aurum Alley. Please, just forgive me and give me an opportunity to fix things!

t_ch: 呵？想放低姿态来博取我的同情？我身为公司的专员，你觉得我的胸膛里还剩下一毫克的同情心吗？起开！
t_en: Huh? Attempting to play the humility card to gain my sympathy? I'm an IPC specialist. Do you honestly think I have even a shred of sympathy left in me? Just get lost already!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…咱们不必把姿态放这么低吧。
t_en: {NICKNAME}... do we really have to stoop this low?

t_ch: 你的诉求呢，我们已经了解了，但目前政策就是这样规定的，我们也没办法改变…不过呢，你的意见我们已经记录了，也会向上级领导反映，有结果会及时通知你的。
t_en: Alright, we understand your position, but rules are rules, and we can't bend them now... However, we'll note down your concerns and pass them on to management. We'll keep you updated on any developments.

t_ch: 哈——？说大声点，我听不太清。能不能再说一遍？
t_en: Huh...? Could you repeat that? I didn't quite hear you. Could you say it again?

t_ch: 你的逻辑太混乱了，我听都听不懂。你能不能先随便找只猴子，和它学学说话的艺术，再回来和我讲这些废话？
t_en: Your logic is too confusing for anyone to follow. Maybe you should learn the art of communication from a monkey before you continue spouting nonsense.

t_ch: 你们几个倒是挺能说会道的……
t_en: Well, you guys sure know how to argue your case...

t_ch: 哎！要执行安全检查也不是不可以。只是…得给我几天时间，毕竟事关重大，让我和公司总部沟通一番。
t_en: Fine! I'll allow you to do the security inspection. It's just that... I need some time to sort things out. This is a big deal. Let me talk to headquarters first.

t_ch: 斯科特先生这是想用「拖」字诀，等拖到舰船修好，天舶司就只能不经检查，放你们离开咯？
t_en: So, Mr. Skott, are you just stalling for time, and planning to leave the Xianzhou as soon as your ship is repaired to avoid the Sky-Faring Commission's inspection?

t_ch: 只要不违犯法律，公司的人当然可以自由来去。
t_en: Well, IPC staff are free to come and go, as long as they don't break any laws.

t_ch: 算你有见识，你是谁？
t_en: Yeah, you've got some insight there. Who are you again?

t_ch: 够了，我讨厌把同样的理由重复说上一百遍。
t_en: Alright, that's enough. I'm not going to repeat myself a hundred times.

t_ch: 原本以为各位是可以讲理的文明人，但现在看来，仙舟人还是不能理解何谓商业精神。
t_en: I thought you were reasonable and civilized, but now it seems you Xianzhou folks just don't understand the basics of business.

t_ch: 我们与博识学会签署过保密安全协议，一旦违约就要支付天价赔偿！就算把这个星区市场开拓部的员工的灵魂全数典当了也填不上这个窟窿。
t_en: We've signed a confidentiality and security agreement with the Intelligentsia Guild, and if we breach it, we'll have to pay a hefty sum! Even if we sold the souls of every employee in the Marketing Development Department on this planet, it still wouldn't be enough to cover the costs.

t_ch: 听好了，小子。我不介意把时间再拖上几日，反正引擎一旦修好，运输船就会离港，你们也就没有理由再进行检查了。
t_en: Listen up, kid. I don't mind delaying this transport for a few more days. Once the engines are repaired, our ship will leave immediately, and you won't have any reason to inspect the cargo.

t_ch: 我向诸位保证，云骑不会放任这艘舰船离开。
t_en: If that's the case, I can assure you the Cloud Knights won't let your ship leave.

t_ch: 那么，到时候会有无数博识学会、公司「市场开拓部」的抗诉接踵而来。你还年轻，你根本不明白自己一个草率的决定会引起如何狼狈的轩然大波。
t_en: Oh, really? Then you'll receive a ton of complaints from the Intelligentsia Guild and the IPC's Marketing Development Department. You're too young, I don't think you understand the chaos your rash decision will cause.

t_ch: 斯科特先生把话说到了这份上，我就当你认同了检查手续。来人啊……
t_en: Since you put it that way, Mr. Skott, I'll take it as your agreement to the inspection procedure. Now...

t_ch: 小子，你最好别太冲动。公司的规章赋予了我武装捍卫运输财产的权力。
t_en: Not so fast, you little brat. According to the IPC regulations, I have the right to arm myself and protect the property on my transport ship.

t_ch: 我原本以为各位是可以讲理的文明人，但看起来公司只想遵守自己的游戏规则。抱歉了，斯科特先生。
t_en: I thought you were reasonable and civilized, but now it seems the IPC just wants to play by its own rules. My apologies, Mr. Skott.

t_ch: 兄弟们，快拦住他！为了琥珀王！
t_en: Stop him, guys! For the Amber Lord!

t_ch: 为了琥珀王！！！
t_en: For the Amber Lord!!!

t_ch: 都给我起来，不许倒下！你们这些废物，对得起公司的薪水吗？
t_en: Get up, all of you! Why is the IPC even paying you? You're a bunch of losers!

t_ch: 你应该感谢你口中那个「小子」手下留情。至少你手下这些人的伤还算有救。
t_en: Just be grateful that the "little brat" showed you mercy. At least your staff aren't seriously injured.

t_ch: 公司到底给了你们多大的底气，竟然对云骑动手…那可算得上「袭击执法人员」了。
t_en: How do you IPC cronies have the audacity to fight Cloud Knights...? You practically assaulted law enforcement officers.

t_ch: 怎么又来了一个？你是谁啊！
t_en: Another one? Who the heck are you!?

t_ch: 来迟了一步。不过好消息是，我来了。
t_en: I may be a bit late, but the good news is: I'm here now.

t_ch: 自我介绍一下，妾身是罗浮丹鼎司司鼎——灵砂。
t_en: Allow me to introduce myself. I'm Lingsha, Cauldron Master and Head of the Alchemy Commission on the Luofu.

t_ch: 莫非她就是…？
t_en: Could she be...?

t_ch: 嗯，她就是那位从朱明仙舟被派来此处的新任司鼎。
t_en: Yeah, she's the new Cauldron Master assigned here from the Xianzhou Zhuming.

t_ch: 工造司发来文书，说有含有不明生物样本的货物滞留此间，需要丹鼎司派人勘验。左右我闲着也是闲着，就亲自来咯～
t_en: I received a report from the Artisanship Commission about cargo containing samples of unknown organisms. It said they needed help from the Alchemy Commission. I had nothing better to do, so I came myself.

t_ch: 斯科特先生，您要是真不打算让我们查验，我们就不查了，无所谓的。
t_en: It's fine, Mr. Skott. If you really don't want your cargo to be inspected, it doesn't matter.

t_ch: 这怎么能说无所谓？
t_en: It doesn't matter? How can you say that?

t_ch: 怎么突然变得这么好说话了？既然你说不查了，那我也要将这个货箱一并带走，没有异议吧？
t_en: Why are you being so nice all of a sudden? Well, since you're not going to check it, I'll take this crate and be on my way. Is that okay with everyone?

t_ch: 异议？妾身能有什么异议？不仅是这个样品，运输船上那些货物，你们也都可以留着，我们不会检查的。
t_en: Yeah, sure. Why should I object? Not only this sample, but all the goods on the transport ship are yours to keep. Like I said, we won't inspect them.

t_ch: 等等！
t_en: Wait a minute!

t_ch: 这才像话嘛！能有这样通人性…我是说通达人情的态度，刚才也犯不着撕破脸。
t_en: Well, that's more like it! If only the young displayed a more... reasonable attitude, we could have sidestepped that altercation just now.

t_ch: 过几日引擎修复完毕，我们的船就会离去。
t_en: Our ship will leave in a few days once the engines are repaired.

t_ch: 船当然可以离去，但货却走不得。
t_en: Your ship can leave whenever you want, but I'm afraid I can't say the same for the cargo.

t_ch: 按照仙舟与公司所签署的进出口法规，一切生物制品，只有确认其对外界不会造成危害或失去生物活性，才能离开港口。
t_en: According to the import and export regulations signed between the Xianzhou and IPC, all biological shipments can only leave the port when they have confirmed to be of no threat, or when all biological activity expires.

t_ch: 不过，既然我们无法判定它是否会造成危害，那就只有等它自己失去生物活性了。按照现有的判例，我想想……
t_en: Since we can't determine if your shipment is safe for the environment, I guess we'll have to wait for its biological activity to expire. Let me check the previous cases...

t_ch: 通常只要四十七个星历年就够了。
t_en: Normally it'll only take around forty-seven star calendar years.
"Only" forty-seven years?

t_ch: 不至于这么惊讶吧？我看你也挺年轻的，中气也足，再活个几十年问题也不大的。对自己有点信心嘛！
t_en: Why so surprised? You're still young and full of energy. I'm pretty sure you'll live a few more decades. Have some confidence in yourself!

t_ch: 不愧是长生种，说起话来夹枪带棒的。你们可以不在乎时间观念，但每一分钟延误所造成的损失，我都要天舶司加倍赔偿！
t_en: Typical of a long-life species, your words are dripping with sarcasm. While you may not care about time, I do! I'll be demanding double compensation from the Sky-Faring Commission for every minute wasted!

t_ch: 看来斯科特先生很有信心，自己的职业和人生都能支撑到见证这场胜利的时刻。
t_en: Sure thing, Mr. Skott. You seem pretty confident that your career and life will last long enough to witness this victory unfold.

t_ch: 兄弟们，都让开吧，让他们查。
t_en: Step aside, guys. Let them do the inspection.

t_ch: 可是，斯科特先生……
t_en: But Mr. Skott...

t_ch: 行了，事情已经够麻烦的了…让他们进行安全检查吧。大不了回去以后我亲自向那些学士们磕头道歉——我这颗脑袋不就是干这个用的吗？
t_en: Come on, we're already in enough trouble... Just let them do the security inspection. And if needed, I can always grovel before the Intelligentsia Guild later — I'm just using my head for what it's apparently good for, right?

t_ch: 给你添麻烦了。
t_en: Sorry for the trouble.

t_ch: 和这个女人一比，也没那么讨人厌了。
t_en: Well, honestly, at least you're not as annoying as that woman.

t_ch: 你还是和过去一样讨厌。
t_en: But you're still as annoying as ever.

t_ch: 谢了，老兄。
t_en: Thanks, bro.

t_ch: 和这个女人一比，也没那么讨人厌了。
t_en: Well, honestly, at least you're not as annoying as that woman.

t_ch: 赶紧搜查吧！谁是你老兄！
t_en: "Bro?" Just do the inspection!

t_ch: 算你识时务。
t_en: At least you've wised up for once.

t_ch: 和这个女人一比，也没那么讨人厌了。
t_en: Well, honestly, at least you're not as annoying as that woman.

t_ch: 赶紧搜查吧！别得了便宜还卖乖！
t_en: Just get on with the inspection! Don't act all chummy with me now that you've got your way.

t_ch: 这位姐姐…还真是不简单啊！
t_en: This lady... is really something else!

t_ch: 你们就要把这种东西在罗浮的码头上放好几天？这也太危险了吧！
t_en: Were you seriously planning to leave this stuff unchecked at the dock for days? That's way too dangerous!

t_ch: 这…这真的不关我们的事！一定是你们刚才不小心触发了「货物」的防卫程序！
t_en: I... I had no idea about it! You must have accidentally triggered the cargo's defense program!

t_ch: 事已至此，还是不要推诿责任更好些。
t_en: I don't think trying to shift the blame is a wise choice.

t_ch: 我是真不知道这东西怎么就动起来了…我可以向琥珀王发誓！
t_en: But seriously, I don't know why that thing suddenly started moving...! I swear on the Amber Lord!

t_ch: 够了。夕葵小姐，麻烦你领几个云骑，先把这几位公司的客人护送到天舶司吧。
t_en: Enough. Miss Xikui, please escort our IPC guests to the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: 明白了。请吧，斯科特先生。
t_en: I'm on it. Please follow me, Mr. Skott.

t_ch: 各位大人，初步查验了一番，确如同匠人担忧的那般，其中藏有生物组织。
t_en: Our preliminary inspection shows that there is indeed hidden biological tissues inside, just like the craftsman feared.

t_ch: 我甚至不确定这东西算是「机巧」还是「生物」。
t_en: I can't even tell if it's ingenium or biological in nature.

t_ch: 为什么这么说？
t_en: Why do you say that?

t_ch: 这东西的核心部分，是黑话里称为「湿件」的东西。说的通俗一些，这台机器是以某种生物的神经作为控制中枢驱动起来的。
t_en: The core of this device is what they call "wetware" in industry slang. To put it simply, this machine operates with a kind of biological nerve as its control center.

t_ch: 我要取走些样本，让丹士们分析分析，就能知道这生物组织的来源。
t_en: I'll take some samples for the alchemists to analyze and figure out where the biological tissue comes from.

t_ch: 博识学会为何会动用这么有悖人伦的技术？为了制造新的武器吗？
t_en: Why would the Intelligentsia Guild use such unethical technology? Perhaps they are trying to create a new weapon?

t_ch: 不管为了什么，这也许正是步离人会袭击这艘舰船的原因吧。
t_en: Whatever the reason is, it's probably why the borisin attacked the ship.

t_ch: 也难怪公司的人不愿意让咱们开箱查验。我会联系十王司，请判官前来权衡判断。
t_en: No wonder the IPC were trying to obstruct our inspection. I'll contact the Ten-Lords Commission and ask the judges to come and give their final verdict on this.

t_ch: 按照规矩，涉及危险生物的囚犯与武器都应当运往幽囚狱收容，再做进一步处理。毕竟那里是整个罗浮最安全的地方。
t_en: According to our rules, all prisoners and weapons involving dangerous creatures must be taken to The Shackling Prison for further sentencing. After all, it's the safest place on the Luofu.

t_ch: 匠人先生，请与云骑同去，向判官说明情况。
t_en: As for you, Mr. craftsman, please go with the Cloud Knights and explain the situation to the judge.

t_ch: 不一会儿，众人各自散去……
t_en: After a while, everyone disperses and goes their own way...

t_ch: 我来时预感到公司的人难缠，只是没想到他们如此难缠。多谢灵砂小姐帮助。
t_en: I had a feeling that the IPC members would cause trouble, but I didn't think they'd be this tricky. Thanks for your help, Miss Lingsha.

t_ch: 我还要感谢您的救命之恩呢，多亏您剑术高超，击倒了这个大家伙。
t_en: I should thank you for saving my life. Your sword skills were impressive, taking down that big guy.

t_ch: 妾身本以为将军的扈从都是昂藏武夫…没曾想，彦卿大人竟然如此……
t_en: I thought the General's retainers were all burly martial masters. I didn't expect Yanqing to be so...

t_ch: 年少。
t_en: Young.

t_ch: 没错，当得一句年少有为。
t_en: Yeah, exactly. He's young and talented.

t_ch: 可爱。
t_en: Cute.

t_ch: 唔，说的不错。
t_en: Hmm, can't argue with that.

t_ch: 浓缩。
t_en: Miniature.
Huh???

t_ch: 至于这三位，想必就是来自星穹列车的客人吧？你们救罗浮于水火之中，好生令人钦佩呀。
t_en: As for you, you must be the guests from the Astral Express, right? Saving the Luofu from that crisis, it's so impressive.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，也没那么厉害啦～
t_en: Hehe, it wasn't a big deal, really...

t_ch: 时间还早，不妨就趁着这个机会，请各位去丹鼎司喝杯茶吧。正好，也让我听听诸位对于丹鼎司重建事宜的建议。
t_en: It's still early, so why don't we get some tea at the Alchemy Commission? We can discuss your suggestions for revitalizing the commission.

t_ch: 那彦卿就恭敬不如从命了。
t_en: I'd be happy to accept your invitation.

t_ch: 你们三位，也要一起来喔。
t_en: And you three are coming too, right?

t_ch: 我们会切断这些机器的动力，在此之前请不要靠得太近了。
t_en: Nothing to see here. These machines are to be shut down, keep your distance until then.

t_ch: 你们这些仙舟人到底讲不讲道理啊？
t_en: Can I really reason with you Xianzhou people?

t_ch: 呵呵，我算是明白了～用你们的话说，这叫「趁火打劫」，对吧？
t_en: Heh, I think I get it now. In your words, this is called "looting a burning house," right?

t_ch: 我现在就是在和你讲道理啊！
t_en: But I AM trying to reason with you here!

t_ch: 怎么还藏了这等惊喜…真是吓煞妾身了。
t_en: Well, how unexpected... that was a surprise~

t_ch: 在下射洪，是龙师涛然派来的信使。龙师听闻族胞升任司鼎，喜不自胜，本想亲自来道喜，可奈何身体状况不允许。
t_en: I'm Shehong, a messenger sent by Preceptor Taoran. He wanted to come and congratulate his kindred on becoming Cauldron Master personally, but health limitations hindered his visit.

t_ch: 因此今日，在下奉命前来，代主家拜谒新任司鼎灵砂大人。不知丹士长大人可否为在下通传一声？
t_en: So, I'm here to pay my respects to the Cauldron Master on his behalf. Could you let her know about my arrival, dear chief alchemist?

t_ch: 灵砂大人才刚到罗浮，生活还未安顿妥当，工作也尚未交接完成，实是繁忙，无暇见客。你请回吧。
t_en: Master Lingsha just arrived on the Luofu and is still settling in and getting her work in order. She's quite busy and unable to meet guests right now. Please understand.

t_ch: 那…这份薄礼，请您代为转交给灵砂大人吧。
t_en: Well then... Please pass this humble gift on to her.

t_ch: 龙师大人的「薄礼」想来相当贵重吧。我一个小小的丹士长，可不敢代为转交，若是中间出了纰漏，我可担待不起啊。
t_en: I'm pretty sure this "humble gift" from the Preceptor himself is quite valuable. As a mere chief alchemist, I am unable to deliver it. Should any mishap occur, I couldn't bear the consequences.

t_ch: 这样啊。那在下何时才能见到灵砂大人呢？
t_en: I see. So when will I be able to see Master Lingsha?

t_ch: 我也不知道。等灵砂大人方便接见时，一定会第一时间联系贵府的，请回吧。
t_en: I'm not sure. Whenever Master Lingsha feels it is appropriate, she will most certainly get in touch with you.

t_ch: 静候佳音。
t_en: Then I shall wait with anticipation.

t_ch: 灵砂大人，您来了。容我提醒一句：在罗浮，龙师与丹鼎司之间关系紧密，这样避而不见，恐怕不符合一直以来的惯例……
t_en: There you are, Master Lingsha. Just a reminder, the Alchemy Commission on the Luofu has always had a close relationship with the Preceptors, so avoiding a meeting like this doesn't align with our long-standing practices...

t_ch: 「惯例」？快算了吧。说的就像罗浮丹鼎司「一直以来的惯例」是什么好东西似的。
t_en: Practices? Come on, you make it sound like the "long-standing practices" in this Alchemy Commission are something great.

t_ch: 司鼎大人所言极是。那…我就继续替您应付他们了。
t_en: You're right, Cauldron Master. In this case... I'll continue handling them on your behalf.

t_ch: 有劳你了。
t_en: Thank you for your help.

t_ch: 你知道我排了多少年的队，才得到请衔药龙女为我看诊的机会吗？到底是什么人插我的队？这合理吗？
t_en: Do you have any idea how many years I've waited just to see the Healer Lady? And now someone cuts in line? How is that fair?

t_ch: 您别生气…这次来看诊的，是一位远道而来的贵客。她实在没办法排上很多年的队，只能在您前面先行问诊了。
t_en: Please calm down... That person is an esteemed guest who has traveled from afar. She simply couldn't wait in line for years, so we had to let her see the Healer Lady before you.

t_ch: 但您放心，只要她看完诊，立刻就到您了。而且那位贵客说了，您此次诊费由她全部承担。
t_en: But don't worry. As soon as she's done, it'll be your turn. By the way, the esteemed guest said she'll cover your medical expenses as compensation.

t_ch: 那…那确实可以接受。你们这位「贵客」倒是个讲道理的好人，我也就不计较了。
t_en: Well... I guess that's acceptable. Your "esteemed guest" seems reasonable, so I'll just pretend nothing happened.

t_ch: 感谢您宽宏大量，请到这边稍事休息。
t_en: Thanks for understanding. Please have a rest over here.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！你回罗浮了啊！怎么也不提前在群里说一声！
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! You're back! Why didn't you say anything in the group chat?

t_ch: 我在执勤，晚点再聊。
t_en: I'm on duty. Let's catch up later.

t_ch: 你严肃的样子好靓女。
t_en: You look so pretty when you're serious.

t_ch: 别闹啊。岗哨神圣，不可侵犯。
t_en: Cut it out. I'm carrying out my sacred duty.

t_ch: 你为什么在这里站岗？
t_en: Why are you standing guard here?

t_ch: 为某个大人物站岗开道。别问了，严肃点，我要执勤。
t_en: Because of someone important. So, stop asking and be serious. I'm on duty right now.

t_ch: 那我不打扰你了。
t_en: Alright, I'll leave you to it then.

t_ch: 好的。再见。
t_en: Yeah, see you later.

t_ch: （做个鬼脸）
t_en: (Pull a funny face at her.)

t_ch: 噗…我警告你，岗哨神圣啊！别跟我嘻嘻哈哈的，烦你了啊！
t_en: *laughs* ...Hey, I'm warning you, I'm carrying out my sacred duty! It'll be annoying if you keep cracking jokes like that!

t_ch: 如果你没别的事，就别在这附近闲逛了。等我过几天忙完了，叫上小桂子和藿藿，一起去金人巷吃个饭吧。好久没聚了。
t_en: Stop hanging around here and find something better to do. Once I'm done in a few days, call up Lil Gui and Huohuo, and let's grab a meal at Aurum Alley. It's been ages since we hung out.

t_ch: 再见！
t_en: Goodbye!

t_ch: 岗哨神圣，不可侵犯！有什么事下了班再说！
t_en: Please do not hinder me from carrying out my sacred duty! We can discuss whatever you want after my shift!

t_ch: 这么多年过去，罗浮丹鼎司的景色依旧未曾变改。
t_en: All these years, and the view at the Alchemy Commission hasn't changed a bit.

t_ch: 古海恒常，潮来潮去，对于我们持明而言，没有比这更值得怀念的故土了。
t_en: The tides come and go, but the ancient sea remains the same. For us Vidyadhara, there's nothing more nostalgic than our homeland.

t_ch: …灵砂小姐是罗浮本土人士？
t_en: ...You're a Luofu native, Miss Lingsha?

t_ch: 是啊，妾身自幼长于罗浮，在丹鼎司中听着这涛声与师长同侪潜心钻研丹方。
t_en: Yes, I grew up here, listening to the sound of waves while researching prescriptions with my mentors and peers at the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 可叹，世事无常又如此相似。我与丹恒先生一样，远游他乡。如今归来目睹旧时景色，心中难免有些感慨。
t_en: It's kind of sad, isn't it? Everything changes but somehow remains familiar. Just like you, Dan Heng, I traveled far from home, and now I've returned. Seeing the familiar scenery brings back a hint of nostalgia.

t_ch: 如果没有建木，这里的景色只怕会更好些。
t_en: The view here would be even better without the Ambrosial Arbor.

t_ch: 是吗？我倒是觉得建木点缀在那里，还挺壮观的。
t_en: Oh, really? I think that towering tree looks pretty impressive.

t_ch: 再壮观也是寿瘟祸迹。仙舟与孽物争斗数千年，看到建木重又升起，人人心底都难免隐然不安的。
t_en: Even if it's impressive, it's a plaguemark. The Xianzhou have been fighting abominations for thousands of years, and now that the Ambrosial Arbor has been reborn, it's only natural for everyone to feel uneasy.

t_ch: 种子一旦被埋下后，无论如何延阻其势，总会有发芽结果的时刻。
t_en: Well, once a seed is planted, no matter how long it takes, it'll eventually sprout and bear fruit.

t_ch: 以妾身的愚见，建木重生，恰如药王秘传再度出现，是不可避免的。这颗种子早在仙舟先民求取长生的时刻就已被埋下了。
t_en: In my humble opinion, the rebirth of the Ambrosial Arbor and the resurgence of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus were inevitable. The seed was already planted when the ancestors of the Xianzhou sought immortality.

t_ch: 怎么突然聊起了严肃话题…
t_en: Why such a serious topic all of a sudden...?

t_ch: 话不可以乱说啊，灵砂小姐。
t_en: We can't just make wild speculations about these things, Miss Lingsha.

t_ch: 不是说喝茶吗？茶呢？
t_en: I thought we were here to have some tea, but where's the tea?

t_ch: 是妾身鲁莽了，丹恒先生与彦卿骁卫是这次建木灾异的亲历者，故而有些事情想要与两位探讨一番。
t_en: My bad. Well, since you went through the entire Ambrosial Arbor Crisis firsthand, Dan Heng and Lieutenant Yanqing, I'd like to discuss something with you.

t_ch: 灵砂小姐想问什么？
t_en: What would you like to discuss, Miss Lingsha?

t_ch: 妾身有幸得到联盟委派，要来清扫这丹鼎司中的积年尘垢。
t_en: I was lucky enough to be chosen by the Alliance to come in and clean up all the old grime in the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 不过这罗浮丹鼎司千疮百孔，早已到了积重难返的地步。我想要开一道「医治良方」，却不知两位有什么高见？
t_en: Honestly, the Alchemy Commission is riddled with problems and has reached a point where fixing it seems impossible. I'm looking to "remedy" this problem, but was wondering if you could provide any insights?

t_ch: 我虽然身为持明族人，但与身边的两位伙伴一样同是外人身份。灵砂小姐想要的「医方」，丹恒无法置喙。但是…有一句忠告，希望灵砂小姐听听。
t_en: Well, even though I'm a Vidyadharan like you, I'm an outsider just like my companions here. I can't really say much about a "remedy," but... I do have a piece of advice, Miss Lingsha.

t_ch: 长久以来，罗浮持明族与丹鼎司有着千丝万缕的合作，若灵砂小姐不能自外其间，想要改变丹鼎司的局面，恐怕很难。
t_en: The Vidyadhara and the Alchemy Commission on the Luofu have always been intricately connected. If you cannot distance yourself from these ties, Miss Lingsha, changing the situation within the Alchemy Commission may be quite challenging.

t_ch: 彦卿对政务懂得不多，只知道药王秘传在丹鼎司中经营多年。灵砂小姐要想革除其影响，可与将军商议对策。
t_en: I may not know about politics, but I do know that the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus have been operating within the Alchemy Commission for years. If you're determined to root them out, maybe you should discuss it with the general.

t_ch: 明白了，两位以真知灼见教我，灵砂在此谢过。
t_en: I see. Thank you for your valuable insights.

t_ch: 帝弓的光矢威力无伦，能斫断建木，却无法断去凡物延续自我的渴望。
t_en: While the Lux Arrow from the Reignbow possesses unparalleled power to sever the Ambrosial Arbor, it can't sever mortals' desire to prolong their existence.

t_ch: 就像云骑，虽然能将药王秘传的乱党余孽铲除，却不能抚平这丹鼎司的人心。
t_en: Just like how the Cloud Knights can eliminate the remnants of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, but are unable to calm the hearts and minds of the people within the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 仙舟先民深知这一节，才会将守望建木玄根的职责付与持明族，但持明也不过是凡物。
t_en: Our Xianzhou forbears knew this well, and that's why they entrusted the duty of guarding the Roots of the Arbor to the Vidyadhara. However, the Vidyadhara are still only mortal beings.

t_ch: 早在三十年前，妾身的授业恩师，也是这罗浮丹鼎司的司鼎，已察觉了此间涌动的暗流，意欲正本清源。
t_en: Thirty years ago, my mentor served as Alchemy Commission's Cauldron Master. She recognized the emerging undercurrents and sought to cleanse the source of the disturbance.

t_ch: 可惜，她虽然精通医术，却并不懂人心，全然不知如何切除潜藏于丹鼎司深处的毒痈。最终，恩师遭人构陷驱逐，远放朱明仙舟。我也受此牵连，不得不离开罗浮。
t_en: Unfortunately, even though she was skilled in the art of healing, she didn't understand the human heart or how to eliminate the sickness lurking within the depths of the Alchemy Commission. In the end, she was framed and exiled to the Zhuming. I was also implicated and had to leave the Luofu.

t_ch: 而当时负责仲裁此事，允可放逐令的…正是景元将军本人。
t_en: And guess who arbitrated that case and handed down the sentence...? None other than General Jing Yuan himself.

t_ch: 什、什么？
t_en: Wh—What!?

t_ch: 你没听错。丹鼎司的局面糜烂至此，要为此负上责任的，除却药王乱党之外，还有神策将军。
t_en: You heard it right. The ones responsible for the corruption in the Alchemy Commission are not just the remnants of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, but even the Divine Foresight himself.

t_ch: 哎呀，彦卿大人怎么脸色都变了？
t_en: Alas, why is your face turning pale, Yanqing?

t_ch: 安心吧，妾身明白「人在其位，身不由己」的道理，绝不会对将军心怀什么怨恨的。毕竟，对我们这个年纪的成年人来说呢，所谓「私人恩怨」，已经是一件很奢侈的事情了。
t_en: Don't worry about it. I understand that when someone holds a position of power, they may sometimes have to make tough decisions. I won't hold any personal grudges against him. Besides, at our age, holding personal grudges is a luxury we can't afford.

t_ch: 灵砂姐姐，你可回来啦！我等了好久！
t_en: Lingsha, you're back! I've been waiting ages for you!

t_ch: 云璃，怎么不在爷爷身边呆着，却跑来丹鼎司闲逛。
t_en: Yunli? Why aren't you with your grandpa? What brings you to the Alchemy Commission?

t_ch: 正好，趁此良机，我来给你介绍介绍，这位彦卿大人是……
t_en: Well, let me take this opportunity to introduce you to Yanqing...

t_ch: 真是冤家路窄啊……
t_en: What a small world...

t_ch: 你，这回你该把偷走的剑还我了吧！
t_en: You...! You stole my sword! Give it back!

t_ch: 我明白了，那就跳过介绍这一步吧。
t_en: I see. Let's skip the "introduction" part then.

t_ch: 怎么在什么地方都能遇到你，你不会是一直跟着我吧？
t_en: Why do I keep bumping into you? Are you stalking me or something?

t_ch: 哼，彦卿自然是有正事要忙，不像云璃姑娘这么闲，有空逛街，却没空还剑。
t_en: Of course not. Unlike you, Miss Yunli, I have important things to take care of. You, on the other hand, seem to have all the time in the world to wander around without returning my sword.

t_ch: 爷爷说过，物肖其主。我和你的剑谈过心了。它告诉我，你心事重重、闲愁万种，该出剑时迟不出剑，不出剑时也持不住剑……
t_en: Grandpa used to say that a sword reflects its master. I talked to your sword, and it told me that you've been distracted. You hesitate when you should strike, and you struggle to stay calm when your sword is unsheathed...

t_ch: 如今再瞧瞧你，我觉得这剑说的不假。不怪我夺走你的飞剑，是你的心思不在剑上。
t_en: Now that I see you again, I realize your sword was right. It wasn't me who took your sword. It was you who lost focus.

t_ch: 你以为我会相信你现编的胡话吗？我敬你是朱明来的客人，才一再忍让，你却毫不领情。难道朱明仙舟没有「拿了别人东西要还」的常识吗？
t_en: Do you really expect me to believe that nonsense? I've been taking it easy on you because you're a guest from the Zhuming, but you're not taking the hint. Don't people from the Zhuming know you're supposed to return what you've borrowed?

t_ch: 你看看这柄飞剑吧，就算我现在还给你，不消一时三刻，它也迟早被人夺了去。
t_en: Just look at this flying sword. Even if I give it back to you now, it'll just be taken away again in a few hours.

t_ch: 「云骑不可令武备脱手」的教条你听过吧？眼下我自然可以还给你，但是上了战场，可就不一样了。哎，这柄飞剑该有多可怜呐。
t_en: You know the Cloud Knight saying: "A Cloud Knight must never let slip their weapon," yes? Well, sure, I can give it back to you now, but on the battlefield, that's a whole different story. Poor flying sword.

t_ch: 你也不必归还了，因为我失去的剑，我会亲自把它夺回来！！
t_en: Fine, you don't have to give it back because I'll take it back myself!!

t_ch: 呼叫将军，呼叫将军…
t_en: Maybe I should call the general...

t_ch: 到底该呼叫哪位将军啊？
t_en: But which general should I call?

t_ch: 你觉得这两位到底谁更厉害一点？别怪我，我是真心好奇。
t_en: Between these two, who do you think is tougher? Don't get me wrong, I'm just curious.

t_ch: 年轻人，真是冲动。
t_en: Oh, to be young and at the whim of impulse.

t_ch: 你觉得这两位到底谁更厉害一点？别怪我，我是真心好奇。
t_en: Between these two, who do you think is tougher? Don't get me wrong, I'm just curious.

t_ch: 做好准备把他们俩分开吧。
t_en: Get ready to separate them.

t_ch: 这就是演武仪典吗？
t_en: So, is this the Wardance?

t_ch: 丹恒，你觉得这两位到底谁更厉害一点？别怪我，我是真心好奇。
t_en: Between these two, who do you think is tougher, Dan Heng? Don't get me wrong, I'm just curious.

t_ch: 做好准备把他们俩分开吧。
t_en: Get ready to separate them.

t_ch: 今天是我在丹鼎司履任的第一日，刀兵见红可不是个开门好彩头。
t_en: It is my first day at the Alchemy Commission — a brawl is definitely not how I imagined celebrating it.

t_ch: 好吧，灵砂姐姐不喜欢，我就不在这里动刀动剑。
t_en: Well, since you don't approve, I won't draw my sword here.

t_ch: 倒也不是这个意思。剑都拔出来了，不让比一比，你们俩谁也不会高兴不是？
t_en: I didn't mean it that way. Since you've already drawn your swords, you'd be disappointed if you didn't get to test one another, right?

t_ch: 我听司里的报告说，丹鼎司洞天周遭至今仍有孽物出没。想来是我的前任们在丹鼎司里留下不少烂账。你们若想比出胜负高下，何必要将刀剑往彼此身上招呼？
t_en: I've received reports that the delves near the Alchemy Commission are still infested with abominations. Seems like my predecessors left quite a mess. So, if you two want to determine who's better, why not focus on them instead of each other?

t_ch: 斩除孽物？啧，真没意思。
t_en: Clearing out some abominations? Meh, sounds boring.

t_ch: 斩杀孽物是云骑分内职责，不必灵砂小姐开口，彦卿会荡平这些孽物。
t_en: It's the Cloud Knights' duty to eliminate those abominations. You don't have to ask me twice, Miss Lingsha. I'll help you get rid of them.

t_ch: 就你会做人？只要灵砂姐姐需要，云璃当然乐意拔剑分忧。
t_en: Oh, you think you're the only one who knows how to behave? If Lingsha needs anything, I'll gladly draw my sword and help her out.

t_ch: 两位小朋友都成了贴心小棉袄，妾身好开心。那咱们走起来？
t_en: It's so heartwarming to see both of you being so sweet and caring. So then, shall we get going?

t_ch: 走吧！
t_en: Let's go!

t_ch: 等等！
t_en: Wait a second!

t_ch: 准备好了吗，各位？
t_en: Are you all ready?

t_ch: 走起！
t_en: Let's do it!

t_ch: 再等等？
t_en: Let's wait a moment.

t_ch: 回来了？你们见过了景元，也到处逛了几个时辰，有何感想？
t_en: Back already? You've met with Jing Yuan and wandered around for a few hours. So, what do you think?

t_ch: 在我看来神策将军是想借演武仪典「示众以强」，证明罗浮在建木灾异之后的局势太平无恙，欣欣向荣……
t_en: It appears that the Divine Foresight is using this Wardance as a show of strength to convince everyone that the Luofu is prospering after the Ambrosial Arbor Crisis...

t_ch: 「不过」…接下来你要说「不过」了对吧？
t_en: But... I know you're going to say "But," right?

t_ch: 不过嘛，随演武仪典所涌入的人群可是不安和流言最大的传声筒。一着棋错，乱象迭起。
t_en: But, the influx of people attending the Wardance is like a breeding ground for disorder and rumors. One wrong move, and the Luofu could be in a world of chaos.

t_ch: 街上的云骑颇为警醒，可见那位将军还是明白这层道理的。至于别的，我瞧不出来。
t_en: The Cloud Knights on the streets remain vigilant, so at the very least General Jing Yuan is aware of this. As for other matters, I'm unable to say.

t_ch: 以后有这等面见将军的好差事，你还是饶了我吧。我一个随军医士怎么就要被推到台前，和两位将军谈笑风生了呢？
t_en: I'd prefer to be excused from future meetings with generals. I'm just a military healer, and now all of a sudden I'm thrust onto the center stage having cordial chats with two generals?

t_ch: 我的工作性质也不适合人前露面。
t_en: My work doesn't lend itself to being in the limelight, either.

t_ch: 别抱怨了，我看你们两个身上也没多几个窟窿眼嘛。
t_en: Just stop whining. At least you're in one piece, right?

t_ch: 在接触之前，我想先摒弃成见，观对方「所成之势」再下判断。这所成之势嘛，自然就是街上云骑的风貌，人们的风评，还有与他亲近之人的行止。
t_en: Before getting in touch with General Jing Yuan, I want to put aside my assumptions and see his "momentum." That includes the overall bearing of the Cloud Knights on the street, what people are saying, and how those close to him behave.

t_ch: 「军之强弱，非在其卒，实在其势。审势之成，乃明强弱。」将军所言，属下受益匪浅。
t_en: "The might of an army dwells not within its pawns, but within the force of its collective momentum. Recognizing this fact reveals the true measure of power." Thanks for enlightening me, General.

t_ch: 好好一句大白话，让你一翻译，我自己都听不懂了。总之，这是我打仗时的习惯，你们也给我习惯习惯吧。
t_en: A perfectly clear statement turned confusing thanks to your translation — you've made me lose where I was now. Anyway, this is how I operate in battle, so you might as well get used to it.

t_ch: 您这是把景元将军当作敌人审视了吗？
t_en: Are you treating General Jing Yuan as your enemy?

t_ch: 罗浮仙舟治军最久的将军，他的敌人，还会少吗？
t_en: The longest-serving general of the Xianzhou Luofu. Do you think he'd have only a few enemies?

t_ch: 对了，将军，你已经见过衔药龙女了吧？能否让我瞧瞧她开出的诊断处方？
t_en: By the way, General, you met the Healer Lady, yes? Could you show me the medicine she prescribed you?

t_ch: 对于我的情况，龙女也无法可施，只是让我「吃点好的」。
t_en: Well, the Healer Lady couldn't do anything about my condition. She just told me to "enjoy some tasty food."

t_ch: …就算是名动一方的衔药龙女，也没法子么？
t_en: ...So, not even the famed Healer Lady could help?

t_ch: 不必担心，我会完成当年的承诺，找到医好你的办法。眼下我已有了眉目。
t_en: Don't worry. I'll fulfill my promise and find a way to cure you. Actually, I've found some leads.

t_ch: 椒丘，生死之事，自有定数。
t_en: Well, life and death, Jiaoqiu, it's all predetermined.

t_ch: 自从军之日开始，我就立下誓愿，余生要成为仙舟的锋镝，射向丰饶孽物。
t_en: Upon starting my military career, I made a pledge that the rest of my life would be dedicated to being the Xianzhou's spearhead, hunting down the Abominations of Abundance till the end of my days.

t_ch: 只要能完成这一夙愿，往后究竟能活多久…我都不在乎。
t_en: As long as I can fulfill that deep-seated desire... I don't care how long I live.

t_ch: 你刚刚说我将景元视作敌人…不，我的敌人从来只有自己。
t_en: You asked if I view General Jing Yuan as my enemy... No, my real enemy has always been myself.

t_ch: 「吃点好的」，所以…晚上我们吃什么？
t_en: "Enjoy some tasty food" So... what's for dinner tonight?

t_ch: 你小子…到底会不会看气氛？
t_en: Geez... you really know how to read the room, don't you?

t_ch: 你们俩自个儿安排吧，我和一位多年没见的老战友有约了。
t_en: You guys figure it out for yourselves. I'm due to catch up with an old war friend I've not seen for quite a long time.

t_ch: 与此同时，彦卿与{NICKNAME}回到了长乐天。
t_en: Meanwhile, Yanqing and {NICKNAME} return to the Exalting Sanctum.

t_ch: 在下来拜谒新任司鼎。
t_en: I'm here to pay my respects to the new Cauldron Master.

t_ch: 灵砂司鼎公务繁忙……
t_en: Cauldron Master Lingsha is busy with official business...

t_ch: 挂了那么多年号才轮到我！
t_en: I've been waiting for years for my turn!

t_ch: 凭什么有人插队！
t_en: And now someone cuts in line!?

t_ch: 这也是没办法的……
t_en: Sorry, but it can't be helped...

t_ch: 很快就好，多多担待。
t_en: Don't worry. Your turn will come soon. Please bear with me.

t_ch: 打从药王秘传覆灭后，他们豢养研究的孽物也被遗落在此，盘踞滋生。
t_en: Ever since the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus were eradicated, their experimental abominations have been festering here.

t_ch: 你们俩若想一较高下，妾身便做个裁判：以一刻为限，看谁在时限内斩除的孽物最多，更胜一筹。
t_en: If you want a contest, I'll be the referee. The one who kills the most abominations within fifteen minutes wins.

t_ch: 灵砂姐姐还是一如既往，教人打白工也说的这么冠冕堂皇……
t_en: Lingsha, as always, you're still an expert in making unpaid work sound so noble and grand...

t_ch: 小云璃，这也是为你们好。你俩有心比出胜负，但刀剑无眼，伤到谁都不好。
t_en: It's for your own good, little Yunli. While you desire to compete against each other, I don't want to see either of you getting hurt.

t_ch: 还是司鼎大人思虑周全。
t_en: That's really thoughtful of you, Cauldron Master.

t_ch: 怎么样？两位准备好了么？
t_en: So, are you both ready?

t_ch: 我前任留下的烂账还真不少啊。
t_en: Looks like my predecessors left quite a mess.

t_ch: 重复一遍，以一刻为限，看谁在时限内斩除的孽物最多，便判谁的剑术更胜一筹。
t_en: Let me say it again: The one who kills the most abominations within fifteen minutes wins.

t_ch: 诸位拔剑时也小心些，切莫伤到了彼此。
t_en: Be careful when you draw your swords, and make sure you don't hurt each other.

t_ch: 灵砂姐姐，可以开始了吗？
t_en: Can we start now, Lingsha?

t_ch: 我赢了，云璃小姐。
t_en: I win, Miss Yunli!

t_ch: 只是侥幸罢了。
t_en: You got lucky.

t_ch: 你还是乖乖把剑还我，恭恭敬敬地向我道歉，然后哭着鼻子回去向爷爷诉苦吧。
t_en: Why don't you just give me back my sword, sincerely apologize, and then go cry your eyes out to your grandpa?

t_ch: 遇上我是你的运气。我对你那把又大又沉、锈迹斑斑的铁剑不感兴趣。我也没有夺人武器，顺手牵羊的爱好。
t_en: Consider yourself lucky that I'm not interested in your rusty sword, as I don't have the nasty habit of snatching other people's weapons.

t_ch: 不过是斩了些怪物就得意忘形。
t_en: All you did was chop down a few monsters. Don't get carried away with yourself.

t_ch: 哼，能不能夺走我手中这把剑，你倒是可以试试啊？
t_en: Heh, if you think you can just take this sword from my hand, go ahead and try.

t_ch: 瞧见没有？是我赢了。
t_en: See? I won.

t_ch: 你的剑招芜杂凌乱，一味图快。罗浮云骑的剑术也不过如此。
t_en: Your technique was all over the place, and you relied too much on speed. Is this really all the Cloud Knights of the Luofu can muster? So disappointing.

t_ch: 不过是斩了些怪物就得意忘形。
t_en: All you did was chop down a few monsters. Don't get carried away with yourself.

t_ch: 哦？那我给你个机会，打败我，或者干脆把你的剑全都交给我算了。
t_en: Oh? Well then I'll give you a chance. Defeat me, or hand over all your swords.

t_ch: 如果挑衅是你的目的，彦卿就奉陪到底！
t_en: If you're trying to pick a fight, just ask, because I'm ready for you!

t_ch: 两位小朋友，打得不错啊。
t_en: Well fought, my young friends.

t_ch: 不过，你们俩一边只知闪躲，自以为身法灵动；另一边力大砖飞，总是妄想一击克敌…战术和技法都有缺漏。
t_en: However, both of you have shortcomings. One of you focuses on dodging and weaving, while the other relies on brute strength, trying to take down targets with a single strike... Both of your tactics and techniques are lacking.

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我嘛，我只是一个来丹鼎司求医问药的病人，顺道路过的看客。
t_en: Me? I'm just a patient seeking medicine from the Alchemy Commission. A passerby, if you will.

t_ch: 原以为罗浮演武仪典召开在即，能在擂台上一饱眼福，却没想到还能在治病救人的丹鼎司里瞧见这么一场精彩的战斗。罗浮之上的怪事还真多啊。
t_en: I thought I'd see my fill of impressive fights during the Wardance — yet here I am, able to witness a remarkable fight at the Alchemy Commission, of all places. Well, the Luofu is never short of surprises.

t_ch: 不过我有个小小的建议，与其在外私斗，我更想看两位小朋友借着演武仪典的机会，上擂台堂堂正正地把对方打个半死，如此一来，什么仇怨都一笔勾销了。
t_en: However, I have a small suggestion for you. Why don't you settle this dispute fair and square in the Wardance's ring? That way, you can resolve your differences with a proper duel, and put your grudges behind you.

t_ch: 仇怨？你误会了，我和云璃…只是切磋罢了。
t_en: Grudges? No, not at all. Yunli and I... we were just sparring.

t_ch: 一个用飞剑，一个抡巨刃，说你们之间没些恩怨，我不信。
t_en: Sparring? You summoned your flying swords, and she swung her sword with full strength. No grudges between you? I don't believe it.

t_ch: 啊呀，飞霄大人，您怎么在这儿？龙女的看诊结束了吗？
t_en: Aha! What brings you here, Lady Feixiao? Have you finished your health consultation with the Dragon Lady?

t_ch: 飞…霄？莫非你就是爷爷时常提起的那位……
t_en: Fei... Feixiao? Grandpa always talks about you... Could it be that you are...

t_ch: …曜青仙舟的天击将军？
t_en: ...The Merlin's Claw of the Xianzhou Yaoqing?

t_ch: 看来我在罗浮仙舟上也还挺有名的嘛。
t_en: Looks like I'm quite famous on the Xianzhou Luofu, too.

t_ch: 那是当然，「大捷将军」的名头，无人不知无人不晓。
t_en: Of course, everyone has heard of the Vanquishing General, known to all, and unbeknown to none.

t_ch: 「大捷将军」？这个称呼未免也太自恋了吧。不成不成！
t_en: Vanquishing General? Isn't that title a bit too narcissistic? I don't like it.

t_ch: 我听说罗浮有个「闭目将军」，所以我也给自己起了个同样谦逊的外号：「三无将军」——无虑，无悔，无敌。
t_en: I heard there's a Dozing General on the Luofu, so I came up with a humble nickname for myself: The "lacking general" — lacking in worries, regrets, and rivals.

t_ch: 这么称呼，是不是听起来好多了？
t_en: Sounds much better, right?

t_ch: 确实是个谦逊低调且不失威风的好名头。
t_en: Yeah, that's a befitting title that sounds both humble and impressive.

t_ch: 彦卿、云璃，既然切磋结束，是不是该有礼貌地感谢飞霄将军的指点，然后安静地握手言和了？
t_en: Now that the sparring session is over, Yanqing and Yunli, shouldn't you politely thank General Feixiao for her guidance and bury the hatchet?

t_ch: …剑，还你，你可得把你的剑看好了，免得下次又被人夺去。还有，这次没分出胜负，下次我会让你输得心服口服的。
t_en: ...Here's your sword. Keep it safe, or it might get taken away again. By the way, we haven't settled the score yet. I'll defeat you fair and square next time we fight.

t_ch: 她这算是道歉的态度吗？拿回了剑，彦卿也该回神策府复命了。飞霄将军，彦卿先行告退。
t_en: This is how she apologizes? Now that I finally got my sword back, I should report to the Seat of Divine Foresight. I'll take my leave, General Feixiao.

t_ch: 对了，灵砂小姐。若有闲暇，彦卿想请您来神策府一叙，与我家将军开诚布公聊一聊。相信今日你所说的「恩怨」，其中一定另有隐情……
t_en: Oh, by the way, Miss Lingsha, if you've got some free time, I'd like to invite you to the Seat of Divine Foresight for a chat with General Jing Yuan. I think there's more to those "personal grudges" you mentioned earlier...

t_ch: 多谢飞霄大人出手了。若是您再晚来些，我已准备点上迷香，麻翻他们两个了。
t_en: Thanks for stepping in, General Feixiao. Otherwise, I'd have had to knock them out with my incense.

t_ch: 不必客气，你请托的事，我办到啦。就当是抵了衔药龙女的诊金如何？
t_en: Not at all, just doing what you asked. How about we call it even as payment for the Healer Lady's consultation?

t_ch: 抱歉，就算贵为将军，也要埋单付钱吧？何况，丹鼎司的医馆从来明码标价，概不赊欠。更何况问诊龙女大人的队伍早已排到了几十年后啦。
t_en: Sorry, but even a general needs to pay their bills. We don't do credit here. And let's not forget, you'd have been waiting decades for a chance to see the Dragon Lady if it weren't for me.

t_ch: 那你把账单寄到神策府上好了，就说是我指点那两个小家伙的教学费用。毕竟方才为了分开他们，我差点动了真格。眼下让我找个地方透透气……
t_en: Well, you can always send the bill to the Seat of Divine Foresight and say it's for mentoring those kids. After all, it was quite the effort splitting them up, I nearly had to get tough. Now, if you'll excuse me, I need to find a spot and get some fresh air...

t_ch: 片刻之后，飞霄信步来到古海滨岸等待…
t_en: A moment later, Feixiao leisurely walks to the ancient seaside shore to wait...

t_ch: 剑都把握不住的家伙，居然有点本事。
t_en: I didn't expect someone who can't even hold onto his sword to actually have some skill.

t_ch: 论剑法，我可不会输你。
t_en: I won't lose to you when it comes to swordplay.

t_ch: 挥舞大剑举重若轻，真不能小看了她。
t_en: She wields her sword with ease. I shouldn't underestimate her.

t_ch: 招数花哨多，又没怎么击中要害。你的剑被这么滥用，真是浪费了。
t_en: You've got style with those moves, but you're not striking where it counts. It's such a shame to see you misuse your sword like that.

t_ch: 看我的！
t_en: Watch this!

t_ch: 给我乖乖趴下！
t_en: Just stay down!

t_ch: 上决浮云，喝——
t_en: Through the clouds, it cleaves a path, Hah!

t_ch: 下绝地纪，灭——
t_en: To earth, it descends with might, eradicate!

t_ch: 上决——浮云——
t_en: Cleave through clouds...

t_ch: 下绝——地纪——
t_en: Descend to earth...

t_ch: 刚才那个从天而降的就是曜青的云骑将军吗？她真的好帅！
t_en: Hey, was that the Yaoqing General who just dropped in out of nowhere? She's so awesome!

t_ch: 云璃挥舞这么大一把剑，她这么轻轻一击，就化解了云璃的攻势——
t_en: I mean, when Yunli swung that massive sword, she just casually blocked her attack with ease...

t_ch: …还有我的。
t_en: ...And mine, too.

t_ch: 咳咳…当然了！作为云璃的对手，那位舞剑的少侠也是英气逼人，身法灵动！看得我都想拜他为师，学习仙舟剑术了！
t_en: Ahem, yeah... But to go up against Yunli is quite impressive, you know? That aura of heroism and grace. It almost makes me want to learn Xianzhou swordplay!

t_ch: 你说是不是啊，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: You think so too, right, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 他真的好帅！
t_en: Yep, those moves were fabulous!

t_ch: 是啊，的确是位极有压迫感的将军。
t_en: I agree. General Feixiao is indeed impressive.

t_ch: 呃，我是说彦卿。
t_en: Uh... I was talking about Yanqing, actually.

t_ch: 嗯，那位将军的确了得…
t_en: Hmmm, that general is totally something else...

t_ch: 是啊，的确是位极有压迫感的将军。
t_en: I agree. General Feixiao is indeed impressive.

t_ch: 我其实希望你夸夸彦卿啊。
t_en: I was actually hoping you'd give Yanqing some praise!

t_ch: 仙舟剑术好哎，真的好！
t_en: Oh, yeah, Xianzhou swordplay is really good! Like really, really good!

t_ch: 给我更有感情一点！
t_en: Can't you put a little more enthusiasm into it?

t_ch: 谢谢三月小姐的安慰。演武仪典召开在即，彦卿侥幸有资格能代表云骑军出战。
t_en: Thanks for the kind words, Miss March. The Wardance is coming up, and I've been chosen to represent the Cloud Knights in the ceremony.

t_ch: 这些日子以来，我不知吃了多少次败仗，早就明白了「天外有天，人外有人」的道理。但今日见识了飞霄将军的身手，心中又开始惴惴不安了。
t_en: I've had my fair share of defeats lately, and even though I know there are always more skilled swordmasters out there, seeing General Feixiao's skills today has made me feel a bit uneasy again.

t_ch: 倒也不必妄自菲薄，毕竟演武仪典将军不会下场。只要牢记你当日以一人之力挑战我和刃时将生死置之度外的心境，便足以战胜大部分挑战者。
t_en: Don't underestimate yourself. After all, generals won't fight in the ring during the Wardance. Just remember the state of mind you had when you single-handedly took on me and Blade, putting life and death aside. With that mindset, you can prevail against most challengers.

t_ch: 我明白了，感谢丹恒老师的指点！今日之事算是告一段落，我家将军想请各位来神策府中小坐，说是有要事相商。
t_en: I see. Thanks for the advice, Master Dan Heng! By the way, now that today's events are over, General Jing Yuan wants to invite all of you to the Seat of Divine Foresight. He has something important to discuss.

t_ch: 要事？
t_en: Something important?

t_ch: 想必是为了商讨如何应付曜青和朱明将军的到来。
t_en: I bet it's about how to deal with the generals from the Yaoqing and the Zhuming.

t_ch: 唉，彦卿不想这么早领略成年人勾心斗角的事情啊…只希望飞霄和怀炎二位将军能明察事实。演武仪典到来前，罗浮之上可不要再生波折了。
t_en: *sigh* I really don't want to get caught up in grown-up games so soon... I just hope Generals Feixiao and Huaiyan can see the truth. We don't need any more chaos on the Luofu before the Wardance.

t_ch: 早些时候在司辰宫，晚辈已向炎老介绍过星穹列车的客人了。不过那时人多耳杂，只能寒暄些琐事。现在，我将三位正式引荐给炎老。
t_en: Earlier at the Palace of Astrum, I introduced these guests from the Astral Express to you, Elder Huaiyan, but with all the people around, we only exchanged pleasantries. Now, I'd like to officially introduce them to you.

t_ch: 这三位甘冒奇险，随景元出入死地，驱逐了首恶幻胧，也揭破药王秘传的阴谋。个中细节，炎老有什么想知道的，尽可以提问。
t_en: These three braved great dangers, accompanying me to perilous places, defeating the chief culprit Phantylia, and uncovering the conspiracies of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus. If you wish to know more, please feel free to ask us.

t_ch: 关于建木重生始末，太卜符玄呈交联盟的一系列报告老朽都看过了。她本人也为此受召前往玉阙接受问话。联盟内部对此事疑虑重重……
t_en: Well, I skimmed through the reports about the Arbor's rebirth from the Master Diviner Fu Xuan. She's been summoned to the Yuque for questioning. There are a lot of doubts within the Alliance about this whole situation...

t_ch: …但是，老朽信得过你。
t_en: ...But despite all that, I believe in you.

t_ch: 你自入行伍起，屡建奇功。「云上五骁」各自隐没后，尽管联盟内对你多有非议，但元帅依然力排众议，将罗浮托付于你。
t_en: Since you joined the ranks, you have repeatedly achieved remarkable feats. After the High-Cloud Quintet each went their separate ways, despite the many criticisms within the Alliance, the Marshal still stood firm against the dissenting voices, and entrusted the Luofu to you.

t_ch: 这些年来，你为联盟竭忠尽智。挫败塔拉萨的孽物，解去玉阙仙舟之围，摧毁丰饶民所唤来的妖星…历次大战中的种种，老朽依旧记得。
t_en: Over the years, you've served the Alliance with loyalty and wisdom. You've taken down abominations in Thalassa, rescued the Xianzhou Yuque from a siege, and destroyed the demonic planet summoned by the Denizens of Abundance... I still remember those battles vividly.

t_ch: 有些蠹虫怀疑你的忠诚，臆断你昏聩无智，他们乐见神策将军失策，是因为他们本性如此，身无建树，只渴望目睹他人的失败作为活下去的养料。
t_en: There are fools who doubt your loyalty. They're happy to see the Divine Foresight fail because it gives them some kind of sick satisfaction. They haven't achieved anything of their own, so they feed off the failures of others.

t_ch: 但老朽见证过太多失败了，我更愿意去相信，相信你的忠诚从未改变。
t_en: But I've seen enough failures in my time. And I want to believe that your loyalty has never wavered.

t_ch: 那么，这次受联盟派遣前来探问建木灾异始末的，只有曜青的飞霄将军了？
t_en: So, General Feixiao of the Yaoqing is the only one investigating the Ambrosial Arbor Crisis on behalf of the Alliance?

t_ch: 怎么会，当然还有我。
t_en: No, no. I am, too.

t_ch: 这位老爷子说话还真是…出人意表。
t_en: This old man's words... always catch me off guard.

t_ch: 元帅发出饬令，要我前往罗浮仙舟。但公文里也不过寥寥几个字：「观礼演武仪典，旁听飞霄问话」。
t_en: The Marshal ordered me to come to the Xianzhou Luofu, but the document only says: "Attend the Wardance and listen to Feixiao's questioning."

t_ch: 这话是什么意思？
t_en: What does that mean?

t_ch: 对元帅而言，她很清楚景元召开仪典的用意，也知晓他所面临的局势。她这么提，显然是认为二者并重。
t_en: The Marshal is well aware of Jing Yuan's purpose in holding the ceremony and understands the situation he is facing. She mentioned it because she believes both issues are important.

t_ch: 元帅的命令？需要回避吗？
t_en: Since it's the Marshal's orders, do we need to step aside?

t_ch: 各位是证人，无妨。在我看来，元帅很清楚景元召开仪典的用意，也知晓他所面临的局势。她这么说，显然是认为二者并重。
t_en: No need for that. You're all important witnesses. The Marshal is well aware of Jing Yuan's purpose in holding the ceremony and understands the situation he is facing. She mentioned it because she believes both issues are important.

t_ch: 炎老襟怀朗照，晚辈铭感五内。但是，元帅交付的饬令，当众说开，这…合适吗？
t_en: Thanks for your kindness and sincerity, Elder Huaiyan, but is it appropriate... to tell everyone here about the Marshal's orders?

t_ch: 你独自向我引介列车的证人，不正是想摸清我与飞霄各自的来意，以及我俩之间是否有所抵牾。老朽赤诚待人，年轻人也就别藏着掖着了。
t_en: By introducing the Express' witnesses to me alone, aren't you aiming to discern the intentions behind both my actions and Feixiao's, and whether there's any discord between us? Well, since I'm being open and honest with you, I encourage you young folk to do the same.

t_ch: 对建木灾异一事，老朽自始至终要做的只有旁听。真正要提出问题的，是飞霄将军。而我更关心的，则是演武仪典能否如期平安举行。
t_en: As for the Ambrosial Arbor Crisis, all I need to do is listen. General Feixiao will be the one asking the questions. To be honest, I'm more concerned about the timely start of the Wardance.

t_ch: 说起来，老朽这次抵达罗浮，为演武仪典带来了一份礼物。
t_en: Oh, by the way, I've prepared a gift for the Wardance.

t_ch: 对，就是这个匣子。
t_en: Yes, it's this case right here.

t_ch: 这张匣子泛着钝光，以非木非金的材质制成。匣面上以高妙的技艺嵌入金属丝络，勾勒出莲花纹样。
t_en: This case gleams with a muted luster, crafted from a material neither wood nor metal. Its surface is masterfully woven with metallic threads, outlining an intricate pattern of lotus flowers.

t_ch: 演武仪典期间，大大小小比武与庆贺数不胜数。其中最重要的莫过于「守擂竞锋」。
t_en: There will be numerous contests and celebrations during the Wardance, and the main event will be the Ringmaster's Challenge.

t_ch: 东道主要派出技艺高超的战士守擂，应对四方挑战，以彰显仙舟武德之盛。当老朽听你提及星穹列车会前来观礼，本以为本次守擂之人会是罗浮龙尊……
t_en: The host will dispatch a skilled warrior to take on challengers from all over the cosmos, showcasing the excellent martial arts of the Xianzhou Luofu. When you mentioned that the Astral Express would be attending the ceremony, I thought the high elder of the Luofu would be the ringmaster...

t_ch: 炎老说笑了。衔药龙女年纪尚浅，又不通武事，怎能让她守擂？
t_en: You humor me, Elder Huaiyan. The Healer Lady is just a young lady who knows nothing about martial arts. How can I assign her as the ringmaster?

t_ch: 论说笑的本事，我还是不及你。
t_en: I'm no match to you when it comes to joking.

t_ch: 这匣子是干什么用的？怀炎将军怎么不把它打开啊？
t_en: What's this box for? Why don't you open it, General Huaiyan?

t_ch: 这是我为演武仪典的奖品准备的剑匣。其中暂时空空如也，但过不了几日，便有一柄宝剑即将抵达此处，收纳在其中。
t_en: This sword case is intended for the Wardance's award. It's empty now, but in a few days, a precious sword will be delivered and stored inside.

t_ch: 不是老朽自夸，此剑是朱明仙舟工造技艺的极致，曾流落异乡，创下种种英雄伟业。个中离奇，实难一语道尽。不过，送来宝剑的使团尚未驾临，我就先在这儿放个剑匣吧。
t_en: I don't mean to boast about our skills, but this sword represents the pinnacle of the Zhuming's craftsmanship. It has a legendary history, full of heroic tales from foreign lands, tales that are too detailed to be summarized in just a few words. Since the delegation delivering the sword hasn't arrived yet, I'll just leave the case here for now.

t_ch: 老朽一直在想…到底什么样的人才能配得上这柄剑呢？思前想后，不如就趁这次守擂竞锋，将它当作奖品送出吧！竞锋胜者想必英雄过人，与宝剑正相匹配。
t_en: I've been wondering... who would be worthy of such a sword? And then it hit me — I can award it to the champion of the Ringmaster's Challenge! The ceremony's champion is sure to be a perfect match for the sword.

t_ch: 而且我听说彦卿小兄弟剑术过人，他代表罗浮守擂，老朽觉得这件礼物真是再合适不过了。
t_en: Moreover, I hear that Yanqing is an excellent swordmaster, and that he will be representing the Luofu as the ringmaster, so it seems like a perfect gift for him.

t_ch: 炎老有此美意，景元就在此谢过了。
t_en: Thank you for your generosity, Elder Huaiyan.

t_ch: 爷爷，想送我剑就直说，何必绕这么大个弯子。
t_en: If you want to give me a sword, just say the word, Grandpa. No need to beat around the bush.

t_ch: 丫头，你倒是自信。可我看你却未必能赢过彦卿呀。
t_en: You've got confidence, my girl, but I don't think you can best Yanqing.

t_ch: 云璃姑娘有爱剑之心，彦卿是知道的。只可惜剑总会选择与它相配的主人。
t_en: I know you're all about swords, Miss Yunli. It's just a shame that it's the sword that ultimately chooses its rightful master.
